methods.description: Process of collecting and preparing genomic data from 426 individuals across 10 distinct populations, utilizing publicly available data and the Indonesian Genome Diversity Project.

steps.description: We examined 426 individuals from 10 distinct populations, taking advantage of publicly available data from previous genomic studies, and a recent effort to sequence hundreds of Indonesian genomes through the Indonesian Genome Diversity Project.
steps.searchable_summary: collect genomic data from 426 individuals using public databases and IGDP

methods.description: Preparation of genomic data including SNP calling, quality control, and phasing, as described in Jacobs et al.

steps.description: For a description of data preparation (SNP calling, QC, phasing) see Jacobs et al.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare genomic data with SNP calling, quality control, and phasing

methods.description: Application of the HMMArchaic method to phased genomic data to identify introgressed haplotypes with high confidence.

steps.description: The model was trained and implemented on phased data, which was obtained as described in Jacobs et al.
steps.searchable_summary: train and implement HMMArchaic model on phased genomic data

steps.description: We used a 1,000bp sliding-window approach, as performed in the original implementation of the method, to allow fine-scale resolution of introgressed fragments.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 1,000bp sliding-window approach for fine-scale resolution of introgressed fragments

steps.description: The HMMArchaic method outputs a posterior probability of introgression for each 1,000 bp window along each chromosome copy of each individual sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate posterior probability of introgression for each 1,000 bp window

steps.description: We decided to drop archaic blocks with posterior probability support <=0.95 to focus on high-confidence introgressed blocks.
steps.searchable_summary: filter archaic blocks with posterior probability <=0.95
